A National Weather Service damage survey team found that a tornado briefly touched down near the intersection of Clito Road and Brooklet-Leefield Road northeast of Brooklet, GA. The tornado is rated EF-0 with max winds of 80 mph.

A NWS survey team confirmed a brief tornado near the Brooklet-Leefield & Clito Road intersection. The tornado briefly touched down in a field along a thin line of trees where several hardwood trees were uprooted. It then traveled across the field to a pine plantation where it uprooted & snapped nearly a third of the trees. The tornado then lifted once it entered the adjacent farm field. The tornado is rated EF-0 with max winds of 80 mph. Path length was 0.19 mile with a path width of 250 yards.

A National Weather Service damage survey team found that a tornado touchdown just northwest of Guyton, GA and travelled southeast across the northern portions of the town before lifting near the intersection of Litle McCall Road and Courthouse Road. The tornado is rated EF-1 with max winds of 90 mph. The preliminary track length is 6.71 miles.

A National Weather Service damage survey team found that a tornado touchdown just northwest of Guyton, GA and travelled southeast across the northern portions of the town before lifting near the intersection of Little McCall Road and Courthouse Road. The tornado is rated EF-1 with max winds of 90 mph. The preliminary track length is 7.09 miles. Path width is 280 yards.

A weak, brief tornado, associated with an outer band of Hurricane Idalia, snapped at least one tree, uprooted at least another tree and destroyed a small wooden shed. No fatalities or injuries reported. Time estimated by radar.

Emergency management reported that a car was flipped over at the intersection of Camelot Drive and South Goose Greek Blvd. Video was used to confirm that the car was hit by a brief, weak tornado. No other damage reported.

A National Weather Service storm survey confirmed a torndo, which was associated with the outer bands of Hurricane Idalia. The tornado touched down just south of Hwy 17 in the White Hall Community of Mount Pleasant. It moved northward across portions of the Carolina Park Community, ending in the northeast portion of the Park West Community, just south of the Wando River. Generally minor tree and some very minor roof damage was found along the path, as well as one car being pushed into a tree.
2 waterspouts reported moving onshore at Folly Beach between 1700 block and old lighthouse.

A National Weather Service storm survey confirmed a weak tornado associated with the outer bands of Idalia. Several trees snapped or uprooted along an approximately 2 mile path in the Francis Marion National Forest. No injuries or fatalities reported. More detailed information to follow in the next few days.

The Bulloch County Emergency Manager reported numerous dirt roads washed out across the northern portions of Bulloch County. Water is also about to enter homes along Northlake and Westlake Drives. Numerous paved roads are also flooded with water flowing across them.
Candler County Emergency Management reporting numerous dirt roads washed out across the county. Water has entered a nursing home in Metter.

A trained spotter provided video of flood waters rushing from a creek and surrounding their house in the 500 block of Willard Street Extension. They stated that water started to enter the garage, laundry room, entryways, and a bedroom at about 6:45 pm. The flood waters started to recede just after 8:00 pm.

Colleton County Emergency Management provided pictures of roadway damage on Cayce Road not far from the intersection with Broxton Bridge Highway. The culvert pipe failed and more than half of that portion of the roadway collapsed.

Dorchester County Emergency Management provided pictures of damage to the roadway on Durhams Corner Road near the intersection with McAlhany Road and near Durhams Store. The roadbed under the eastbound lane near a small creek was severely eroded and the road compromised.
Smith St dirt road completely washed out near Hickory Hill Rd.
A 55 acre pond dam failed on Cook Pond Rd.
2 vehicles swept off the road by moving floodwaters on Indian Field Circle near HWY 15. Both drivers were able to escape their vehicles on their own.
A lifeguard and member of the general public reported a waterspout that was north of Folly Beach near Bowen Island. It was on the water for about five minutes, then dissipated. No damage reported.
Dorchester County EM provided pictures of wind damage within the 100 block of Mockingbird Drive. The pictures indicated one downed trees with multiple large limbs down.

Video shared via social media shows water entering a business along South Market Street.

At least one home was flooded on Mercury Road, the homeowner needed to be rescued. The entire area along the roads of Mercury, Hamlin, and Atlanta Drive was flooded with a combination of salt and fresh water. An estimated 30-40 homes were surrounded by floodwaters with the occupants sheltering in place.
Video relayed via social media shows water inside a building along Drake Street.
*** 1 Fatal *** Broadcast media reports a drowning occurred off of Omni Blvd. The victim was a 72 year old woman. The car was fully submerged in flood waters and she was trapped inside.
*** 1 FATAL *** Broadcast media reports a drowning occurred off of Omni Blvd. The victim was a 72 year old woman. The car was fully submerged in flood waters and she was trapped inside.
Water entered several businesses at South Windermere Shopping Center.
At least 2 businesses in McClellanville were reported to have had at least 6 inches of water inside the building. One was on Pinckney Street and another was on Morrison Street.

Significant flooding of Old Georgetown Road was reported on the McClellanville/Awendaw Town Forum Facebook page. Pictures and video show water still flowing across the roadway. One shoulder of the road is completely inundated and the edge of the asphalt is damaged and eroded away.
The town of McClellanville has reported that Old Cemetary Road is washed out just before the bridge over Jeremy Creek. This leaves around 100 people cut off from the main road.
